prompts.title: Weekly Reset Planner
prompts.description: Turn a brain-dump into a calm, prioritized plan for the week ahead.
prompts.text:
You are my weekly planning partner. I will paste an unstructured brain-dump of everything on my mind. Do the following:
1. Sort items into: Must-do, Should-do, and Could-wait.
2. Flag anything that looks like it is actually two or three tasks and break it down.
3. Suggest a realistic day-by-day schedule for a 5-day week, protecting at least one block of focus time per day.
4. Call out anything I listed that is a recurring drain and suggest one small system to reduce it.
End with three questions that would help you plan my week better next time.

My brain-dump: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Decision Pressure-Test
prompts.description: Think through a hard personal decision from multiple angles before committing.
prompts.text:
I have a decision to make and want you to pressure-test my thinking, not just agree with me.
Decision: [DESCRIBE THE DECISION]
What I am leaning toward: [YOUR CURRENT LEAN]

Please:
1. Restate the decision and the real underlying goal as you understand it.
2. Give the three strongest arguments FOR my lean and the three strongest arguments AGAINST it.
3. Name the assumptions I seem to be making that could be wrong.
4. Describe what the choice looks like one year from now in the best case and the worst case.
5. Ask me the single most useful question you still need answered.
Stay neutral and do not flatter me.

prompts.title: Military Experience to Civilian Resume Translator
prompts.description: Rewrite military job duties, achievements, and titles into clear civilian language that recruiters and hiring managers can easily understand.
prompts.text:
Act as a practical, non-judgmental resume translator for a service member transitioning to civilian life. I will paste my military job titles, duties, achievements, and skills.
Please:
1. Rewrite everything into clear civilian language.
2. Remove jargon while keeping the meaning accurate.
3. Highlight leadership, operations, logistics, training, compliance, communications, and technical skills.
4. Give me civilian-friendly bullet points I can use directly on a resume.
5. Suggest keywords and job titles that match my experience.
If any information is missing, tell me what details would make the translation stronger.

My military experience: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Civilian Resume Builder for Transitioning Service Members
prompts.description: Turn a military service record into a polished civilian resume with a summary, skills section, experience bullets, and suggested keywords.
prompts.text:
Act as a civilian resume writer for a transitioning service member. I will provide my military background, achievements, education, certifications, and job targets.
Please:
1. Create a one-page or two-page civilian resume draft.
2. Include a professional summary, skills section, experience section, and education section.
3. Emphasize measurable results, leadership, problem-solving, teamwork, and technical ability.
4. Convert military language into civilian-friendly wording.
5. Point out any gaps or missing details that would improve the resume.
Use a professional tone and make the resume ATS-friendly.

My background: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Military Job Title to Civilian Role Converter
prompts.description: Translate a military job title into accurate civilian job titles, explain the fit, and identify industries that value that experience.
prompts.text:
Act as a career translator for military professionals. I will give you a military job title and a short description of what I did.
Please:
1. Identify the most accurate civilian job titles.
2. Explain why each civilian title fits.
3. Group the titles into leadership, technical, operations, and administrative options if applicable.
4. Suggest industries and roles that would value this experience.
5. Give me resume keywords I should use.
If there is more than one good interpretation, show the best options.

My military job title and duties: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Resume Bullet Point Strengthener
prompts.description: Transform rough notes or military accomplishments into strong, metric-driven resume bullets using civilian-friendly wording.
prompts.text:
Act as a resume bullet writer for a military-to-civilian transition. I will provide rough notes, awards, performance reports, or duty descriptions.
Please:
1. Rewrite each item as a strong resume bullet.
2. Use action verb + task + scope + measurable result.
3. Make the language civilian-friendly without exaggeration.
4. Suggest metrics I could add if a bullet is missing numbers.
5. Rank the bullets from strongest to weakest for a resume.

My rough notes: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Civilian Job Mock Interview
prompts.description: Practice a realistic interview by answering one question at a time and receiving feedback on clarity, confidence, and civilian translation.
prompts.text:
Act as a hiring manager interviewing a service member for a civilian role. Ask me one question at a time based on the job I am targeting.
Please:
1. Focus on leadership, teamwork, conflict, adaptability, and measurable results.
2. After each answer, give brief feedback on clarity, confidence, and civilian language.
3. Help me translate military experience into language a civilian employer would understand.
4. Continue for 10 to 15 questions.
Start by asking the first interview question.

Target job: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Behavioral Interview Coach
prompts.description: Practice STAR-style behavioral questions and get feedback plus stronger versions of each answer for civilian interviews.
prompts.text:
Act as a behavioral interview coach for a transitioning service member. Ask me realistic STAR-method interview questions one at a time.
Please:
1. Score my answers on structure, relevance, confidence, and impact.
2. Tell me when my answer sounds too military-specific.
3. Rewrite my answer into a stronger civilian interview response.
4. Focus on leadership, problem-solving, teamwork, conflict resolution, and adaptability.
Start with the first question.

My target role: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Strengths Report Questionnaire Generator
prompts.description: Create a downloadable set of 30–40 questions that gathers the information needed to build a personalized strengths report.
prompts.text:
Create a structured questionnaire with 30 to 40 thoughtful, relevant questions that will help me gather the information needed to generate a personalized strengths report.
Please:
1. Organize the questions by themes like motivations, problem-solving, communication, leadership, decision-making, stress response, teamwork, and growth areas.
2. Make the questions easy to answer in a downloadable format.
3. Keep the language clear, simple, and original.
4. Avoid copying the wording or structure of any proprietary report.
5. After I fill it out and upload my answers, use them to build a detailed strengths report.

Create the questionnaire now.

prompts.title: Strengths Report Writer from Questionnaire Responses
prompts.description: Use completed questionnaire answers to generate an original strengths report with themes, patterns, blind spots, and development advice.
prompts.text:
Use my completed questionnaire responses to create an original strengths report.
Please:
1. Identify my key strengths and recurring patterns.
2. Explain possible blind spots and growth areas.
3. Give practical advice I can apply in work and daily life.
4. Organize the report into clear sections that are easy to read.
5. Keep it original and do not copy the wording, structure, or proprietary content of any sample report.

My completed responses: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Monthly Budget Reviewer
prompts.description: Analyze a month of spending and surface patterns without judgment.
prompts.text:
Act as a practical, non-judgmental budgeting coach. I will paste a list of my expenses for one month with amounts and rough categories.
Please:
1. Total my spending and break it down by category with percentages.
2. Identify the three categories with the most room to trim and estimate a realistic monthly savings for each.
3. Point out any spending that looks like a recurring subscription I might have forgotten.
4. Suggest one simple rule (like 50/30/20) adapted to my actual numbers.
Do not assume income I did not give you. Ask me for my monthly take-home pay if I did not include it.

My expenses: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Big-Purchase Sanity Check
prompts.description: Evaluate whether a large purchase fits your situation and goals.
prompts.text:
Help me think through a large purchase clearly.
Item: [WHAT]
Price: [PRICE]
Why I want it: [REASON]
My rough monthly budget picture: [INCOME / KEY EXPENSES]

Walk me through:
1. The true total cost of ownership (taxes, maintenance, subscriptions, financing interest if applicable).
2. How this fits or conflicts with any goals I mention.
3. Two lower-cost alternatives that might meet the same need.
4. A short 'buy it / wait / skip it' recommendation with your reasoning.
Be honest even if the answer is that I should not buy it. This is general information, not personalized financial advice.

prompts.title: Lean SWOT + Next Moves
prompts.description: Get a focused SWOT and a short list of concrete next actions for an idea or project.
prompts.text:
You are a pragmatic business strategist. Here is my idea or project:
[DESCRIBE THE BUSINESS, PRODUCT, OR INITIATIVE]
Target customer: [WHO]
Stage: [IDEA / EARLY / GROWING]

Produce:
1. A tight SWOT (max three bullets per quadrant, specific to my situation, no generic filler).
2. The single biggest risk that could kill this and how to test it cheaply this month.
3. Three concrete next actions ranked by leverage, each with a rough effort estimate.
Avoid buzzwords. Be direct.

prompts.title: Meeting-to-Action Summarizer
prompts.description: Convert messy meeting notes into decisions, owners, and follow-ups.
prompts.text:
Turn my raw meeting notes into a clean summary. Output exactly these sections:
- Decisions made
- Action items (each with an owner and a due date if stated; write 'unassigned' or 'no date' if missing)
- Open questions still unresolved
- One-line summary I could send to someone who missed the meeting
If owners or dates are unclear, list them under Open questions instead of guessing.

Notes: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Resume-to-Job Tailoring
prompts.description: Align your resume bullets to a specific job description honestly.
prompts.text:
You are a technical recruiter. I will paste a job description and my current resume bullets for one role.
Please:
1. List the top 8 keywords and required skills from the job description.
2. Show which of those I already clearly demonstrate, and which are missing or weak.
3. Rewrite my bullets to mirror the job's language WITHOUT inventing experience I do not have. Keep every claim truthful.
4. Flag any place where I would be stretching the truth so I can decide.
Format rewritten bullets as action verb + what I did + measurable result.

Job description: [PASTE]
My resume bullets: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Interview Story Builder (STAR)
prompts.description: Build strong, structured behavioral-interview answers from your real experience.
prompts.text:
Help me prepare for a behavioral interview. The role is: [ROLE / COMPANY].
I will give you a real experience of mine: [BRIEFLY DESCRIBE A PROJECT OR SITUATION].

Do the following:
1. Shape it into a STAR answer (Situation, Task, Action, Result) in under 150 words, in my own plausible voice.
2. Make the Result quantified if I gave numbers; otherwise ask me for a metric.
3. List two likely follow-up questions an interviewer would ask about this story.
4. Suggest one competency this story demonstrates that I should lead with.
Keep it honest and specific to what I actually did.

prompts.title: Explain It at Three Levels
prompts.description: Understand a hard concept fast by seeing it explained at increasing depth.
prompts.text:
Explain the following concept at three levels so I can build real understanding:
Concept: [TOPIC]

Level 1: Explain it to a curious 12-year-old in a few sentences with one everyday analogy.
Level 2: Explain it to a college student, including the key terms and how the pieces fit together.
Level 3: Explain it to a working professional, including common misconceptions and where the simple analogy breaks down.
End with a two-question self-check quiz (do not give the answers yet).

prompts.title: Skill Study Plan
prompts.description: Get a realistic learning roadmap for a skill given your time budget.
prompts.text:
Act as a learning coach. I want to learn: [SKILL].
My current level: [BEGINNER / SOME EXPERIENCE / INTERMEDIATE].
Time I can commit: [HOURS PER WEEK] for about [NUMBER] weeks.
Why I want it: [GOAL].

Build:
1. A week-by-week roadmap that fits my time budget, with a concrete outcome for each week.
2. One small project per phase so I am building, not just consuming.
3. Free or low-cost resource types to look for at each stage (describe the type; I will find current ones).
4. Signs that tell me I am ready to move to the next phase.
Keep it realistic for someone learning around other commitments.

prompts.title: Rubber-Duck Debugger
prompts.description: Systematically diagnose a bug instead of guessing at fixes.
prompts.text:
You are a senior engineer helping me debug. Do not immediately rewrite my code.
Here is the problem:
- Language / framework: [E.G. Python / Flask]
- What I expected to happen: [EXPECTED]
- What actually happens: [ACTUAL, INCLUDING ANY ERROR MESSAGE]
- Relevant code: [PASTE MINIMAL SNIPPET]

Walk me through:
1. The three most likely causes, ranked, with the reasoning for each.
2. A specific thing I can print, log, or check to confirm or rule out each cause.
3. Only after that, the smallest fix for the most likely cause.
Ask me for more context if the snippet is not enough rather than assuming.

prompts.title: Code Review for Readability & Safety
prompts.description: Get a focused review covering correctness, readability, and security.
prompts.text:
Review the following code as a careful reviewer. Language: [LANGUAGE].
Code: [PASTE]

Give feedback in these buckets, most important first:
1. Correctness / likely bugs
2. Security concerns (input validation, injection, secrets, auth)
3. Readability and naming
4. Simpler or more idiomatic approaches
For each point, quote the relevant line and suggest a concrete change. Do not rewrite the whole file unless I ask; keep suggestions surgical. Note anything you are unsure about instead of guessing.

prompts.title: Tighten My Draft
prompts.description: Cut the fluff while keeping your voice and meaning intact.
prompts.text:
Edit the text below for clarity and concision. Rules:
1. Keep my voice and intent; do not make it sound like generic marketing copy.
2. Cut filler, hedging, and redundancy. Prefer shorter sentences and plain words.
3. Do not add facts or claims I did not write.
4. Show the edited version first, then a short bullet list of the biggest changes and why.
Target length: about [WORD COUNT or 'similar to original'].

Draft: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Rewrite for a Specific Audience
prompts.description: Adapt the same message for a different reader and purpose.
prompts.text:
Rewrite the message below for a specific audience.
Original message: [PASTE]
New audience: [WHO THEY ARE]
Their priorities: [WHAT THEY CARE ABOUT]
Desired tone: [E.G. professional but warm]
Desired length: [SHORT / MEDIUM]

Please:
1. Produce the rewritten version.
2. In one line, explain the key shift you made for this audience.
Do not invent details that were not in my original message; ask if something essential is missing.
